tickets.title: US001 – Place Order (Student)
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to place food orders on my phone, so that I can avoid waiting in long queues.

Acceptance Criteria:
User can browse menu on mobile
User can place an order successfully
Order is sent to kitchen system in real-time

tickets.title: US002 – Allergy Filtering (Student)
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to filter menu items based on allergens, so that I can avoid foods that are unsafe for me.

Acceptance Criteria:
User can select allergen preferences (e.g., peanuts)
Items containing allergens are hidden completely
Filter applies across full menu

analysis.ambiguity1: "We need to reduce the queue." : This requirement is vague because it does not define what "reduce" means in measurable terms. It is unclear whether the goal is to reduce waiting time, number of people in line, or average service time, and no target metric is provided.
analysis.ambiguity2: "Send push notifications at 11:30 AM offering a discount." : This requirement is ambiguous because it does not specify user preferences, notification frequency limits, opt-out options, or whether notifications apply to all users or selected groups.
analysis.conflictSolution:
Conflict:
The Chef needs to mark items as "Sold Out" instantly, but the Cafeteria Manager wants real-time ordering from mobile devices. There is a risk that students may still order items that are no longer available.

Proposed Solution:
The system should implement real-time inventory synchronization. Once an item is marked "Sold Out", it must immediately be removed from the ordering interface. Any pending orders for that item should be blocked and users should be notified instantly.
analysis.nfr1:
Performance:
The system must process and display orders in real-time to reduce queue waiting time.
analysis.nfr2:
Reliability:
The kitchen display system must continue functioning offline for at least 10 minutes and automatically sync when the connection is restored.
analysis.nfr3:
Usability:
The application must support allergy-based filtering that completely hides restricted items from the user interface.
